{"id":"project-LICHEN-fss7","title":"senml: skip_one_depth rejects CBOR simple values 32-255","description":"file: rust/lichen-senml/src/cbor.rs:403-424

The skip_one_depth function handles major type 7 (simple/float) but misses info=24, which encodes simple values 32-255 as a 2-byte sequence (0xf8 0xXX).

Current handling:
- info 20-23: Ok(1) - false, true, null, undefined
- info 25: Ok(3) - half-precision float
- info 26: Ok(5) - single-precision float  
- info 27: Ok(9) - double-precision float
- other: Err(InvalidInput) <-- includes info=24

CBOR simple values 32-255 are valid (RFC 8949) and would be encoded as `0xf8 0xXX` (2 bytes). If encountered as an unknown map value, skip_one would fail with InvalidInput.

Fix: Add case for info=24 returning Ok(2).

Impact: Valid CBOR containing simple values 32-255 in unknown fields would cause decode to fail.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T17:16:46.151928Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T17:16:46.151961Z"}

new_value: Fixed skip_one_depth for CBOR major=7 info=24 (simple values 32-255)
